title: Ethnic neighborhood
content:
{{Top|leg}}
'''Ethnic neighborhoods''' were zones in [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] for its nonhuman inhabitants by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] as part of it [[Humanocentrism|humanocentric]] policies.

==History==
Alien species from arctic worlds would be segregated to the planet's colder altitudes while those from hot, tropical worlds were confined to the equator. Those accustomed to subterranean settings were confined to the lower, shadowy levels beneath the towering skyscrapers. Though these ethnic neighborhoods were officially to protect alien residents from supposed "genuine prejudice," they were in reality part of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Humanocentrism|humanocentric]] policies.

Conditions inside such places were very poor and the residents were often the targets of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]]. According to an Imperial propaganda report by [[Pollux Hax/Legends|Pollux Hax]], architecture varied from neighborhood to neighborhood to reflect the various different cultures of the galaxy's alien species, though this may have been untrue. To support Imperial propaganda claims that the alien residents were being protected from prejudice, they were dominated by statues to honor alien heroes from the homeworlds of the residents. A notable ethnic neighborhood was the [[Alien Protection Zone/Legends|Alien Protection Zone]], also known as the Invisible Sector (Invisec).

==Appearances==
*''[[Coruscant Nights I: Jedi Twilight]]''
*''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble (unabridged audiobook)}}
